query: If I had a wind asset in ERCOT West and a solar asset with the same capacity in CAISO SP15 zone in 2024, which one would make me the most merchant revenue assuming real time market prices only and no curtailment in both regions. Use revenue numbers in $/MWh for the comparison
final_answer:
All the data is in. Here is the full, detailed analysis:

---

# 🌬️ ERCOT West Wind vs. ☀️ CAISO SP15 Solar — 2024 Merchant Revenue Analysis

## Methodology & Assumptions
| Parameter | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|---|---|---|
| Location | Midland, TX (31.997°N, -102.078°W) | Lancaster, CA (34.68°N, -118.14°W) |
| Generation Profile | Renewables.ninja – Vestas V90 2MW @ 80m hub | Renewables.ninja – 1 kW, 20° tilt, south-facing |
| Prices | ERCOT RT 15-min SPP `LZ_WEST` (GridStatus) | CAISO RT 15-min LMP `TH_SP15_GEN-APND` (GridStatus) |
| Year | Full calendar year 2024 (8,784 hours) | Full calendar year 2024 (8,784 hours) |
| Normalized to | 1 MW nameplate | 1 MW nameplate |
| No curtailment assumed | ✅ | ✅ |

---

## 📊 Head-to-Head Summary

| Metric | 🌬️ ERCOT West Wind | ☀️ CAISO SP15 Solar |
|---|---|---|
| **Annual Generation** | **3,681 MWh/MW** | **1,868 MWh/MW** |
| **Annual Capacity Factor** | **41.9%** | **21.3%** |
| **Flat Average RT Price** | $35.69/MWh | $30.99/MWh |
| **🔑 Generation-Weighted Realized Price** | **$28.31/MWh** | **$7.32/MWh** |
| **Price Capture Rate** | **79.3% of flat avg** | **23.6% of flat avg** |
| **CF vs. Price Correlation** | -0.142 (mild) | -0.496 (severe) |
| **Annual Revenue/MW** | **$104,206/MW** | **$13,665/MW** |

> **🏆 Winner: ERCOT West Wind by a massive margin — $104,206/MW-yr vs. $13,665/MW-yr, nearly 7.6× more revenue.**

---

## 💡 Why the Realized $/MWh Tells the Full Story

The relevant comparison for a merchant asset is **generation-weighted realized price** ($/MWh), not the flat average price:

| | Wind | Solar |
|---|---|---|
| **Flat avg market price** | $35.69/MWh | $30.99/MWh |
| **Realized merchant $/MWh** | **$28.31/MWh** | **$7.32/MWh** |
| **Discount to flat** | -21% | **-76%** |

**Solar massively undersells itself** because it generates exactly when the market is flooded with solar — a textbook **price cannibalization / merit order effect**.

---

## 🗓️ Seasonal Realized Prices ($/MWh)

| Season | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|---|---|---|
| **Winter** | $24.85 | $17.90 |
| **Spring** | $28.87 | **-$21.63** ⚠️ |
| **Summer** | $30.90 | $21.79 |
| **Fall** | $28.54 | $15.96 |

Spring in CAISO SP15 was catastrophic for solar — the duck curve was deepest, with massive afternoon solar oversupply driving prices deeply negative precisely when solar output was highest.

## 📅 Monthly Realized Prices

| Month | Wind ($/MWh) | Solar ($/MWh) |
|---|---|---|
| Jan | $25.82 | $29.17 |
| Feb | $15.15 | **-$0.85** |
| Mar | $31.44 | **-$21.07** |
| Apr | $18.22 | **-$25.99** |
| May | $37.88 | **-$18.16** |
| Jun | $23.65 | $9.32 |
| Jul | $24.68 | $31.86 |
| Aug | $46.65 | $24.45 |
| Sep | $30.84 | $18.48 |
| Oct | $25.15 | $20.24 |
| Nov | $30.44 | $7.35 |
| Dec | $35.79 | $25.28 |

---

## 🔑 Key Drivers of the Gap

### 1. Capacity Factor (Volume Effect)
ERCOT West is one of the best wind regions in the US. At **41.9% CF**, the wind asset generates **~2× more energy** per MW of capacity than the solar asset (21.3% CF). This alone roughly doubles revenue.

### 2. Severe Solar Price Cannibalization in CAISO (Price Effect)
- CAISO SP15 solar prices during **generation hours averaged only $16.36/MWh**, vs. **$44.57/MWh at night** — a 63% discount.
- **1,183 generation hours** saw negative prices in 2024 (averaging **-$22.09/MWh**), costing revenue in the Spring especially.
- CF-vs-price correlation of **-0.496** for solar (vs. -0.142 for wind) confirms severe structural price depression.

### 3. ERCOT West Wind Has Better Price Correlation
Wind in West Texas correlates far less negatively with prices. August saw a **$46.65/MWh** realized price — winds pick up in the evening/nighttime when solar is offline, and ERCOT West can sometimes benefit from scarcity events. Only 687 generation hours went negative (avg -$3.35/MWh), a far smaller drag than solar.

---

## 📝 Caveats
- Revenue figures are **pre-curtailment** as requested (real-world ERCOT West wind curtailment runs ~10-20%, which would reduce wind revenues further).
- No transmission costs, ancillary services, or capacity payments are included.
- Wind profile uses a Vestas V90 2MW turbine as a representative West Texas unit; actual asset revenue depends on specific turbine and location.
- Sources: [Renewables.ninja](https://renewables.ninja) for generation profiles; [GridStatus.io](https://gridstatus.io) for ERCOT RT SPP `LZ_WEST` and CAISO RT LMP `TH_SP15_GEN-APND`.

steps.content: Great question! I'll need to pull wind generation profiles, solar generation profiles, and real-time market prices for both regions. Let me kick off all the data collection simultaneously.
steps.reasoning:
The user wants to compare merchant revenue from:
1. A wind asset in ERCOT West (2024)
2. A solar asset in CAISO SP15 zone (2024)

I need to:
1. Get wind generation profiles for ERCOT West location
2. Get solar generation profiles for CAISO SP15 zone
3. Get real-time market prices for both ERCOT West and CAISO SP15 in 2024
4. Calculate revenue = generation (capacity factor) × price for each hour
5. Compare on a $/MWh basis

Let me start by:
- Getting coordinates for ERCOT West and CAISO SP15
- Pulling wind and solar generation data from Renewables.ninja
- Fetching real-time market prices from GridStatus

For ERCOT West, I'll use a representative West Texas location around the Midland/Odessa area, and for CAISO SP15 (Southern California), I'll pick a location that's representative of solar resources, somewhere in the Mojave Desert region or near Los Angeles.

Now I need to geocode these coordinates and retrieve the GridStatus datasets for real-time LMPs—specifically the West Hub settlement point for ERCOT and the SP15 node for CAISO.

steps.content: Lancaster, CA was misidentified — let me fix that and simultaneously inspect the key price datasets and kick off the generation profiles!
steps.reasoning:
The geocoder returned Lancaster, PA instead of Lancaster, CA. I need to get the correct coordinates for Lancaster, CA (which is in the SP15 zone of CAISO). Let me also inspect the relevant datasets and start pulling price data simultaneously.

For ERCOT West wind, I'll use:
- ERCOT SPP Real Time (for West hub prices)
- Wind profile from Renewables.ninja for Midland, TX

For CAISO SP15 solar:
- CAISO LMP Real Time (for SP15 prices)
- Solar profile from Renewables.ninja for Lancaster, CA area

Let me get:
Now I need to verify the correct geocoding for Lancaster, CA and pull the real-time price data from both ERCOT SPP and CAISO LMP datasets.

steps.content: Now I have the dataset details. Let me check what locations are available in each price dataset for the SP15 and ERCOT West zones, while simultaneously launching generation profiles:
steps.reasoning:
Good. Now I have the datasets. Let me:
1. Get the correct CAISO SP15 hub location name
2. Get the correct ERCOT West location name
3. Get wind generation profile for ERCOT West (Midland, TX area)
4. Get solar generation profile for CAISO SP15 (Mojave Desert / Lancaster, CA area)

For CAISO SP15, let me use coordinates in the Mojave/Antelope Valley area. Let me use approximately 34.7°N, -118.1°W (Antelope Valley/Lancaster, CA area) which is well within SP15.
I'll use Midland, TX coordinates for the ERCOT West wind data and query the price data for HB_WEST and SP15 zones, along with generation profiles for both locations. I realize hourly resampling will keep the row count manageable—2024 has 8784 hours, which easily fits within the limit. I'll pull all four datasets at once: ERCOT West and CAISO SP15 prices at hourly resolution, plus wind data for Midland, TX and solar data for the Lancaster area in the SP15 zone around 34.68°N, -118.14°W.

steps.content: I can see the SP15 CAISO hub is `TH_SP15_GEN-APND`. Now let me pull the full year 2024 hourly price data for both regions simultaneously, and also confirm ERCOT West load zone name:
steps.reasoning:
Great! I can see:
1. ERCOT's "ERCOT West" Load Zone is likely "LZ_WEST" - I need to find the right location name. Let me now query for the ERCOT West load zone price data and CAISO SP15 price data for the full year 2024. The CAISO SP15 location is "TH_SP15_GEN-APND".

For ERCOT, "LZ_WEST" should be the West load zone. Let me confirm and get hourly prices.
I'm calculating the data volume needed - 2024 is a leap year with 8,784 hours, so at 15-minute intervals that's about 35,000 rows for ERCOT, which fits within the 50,000 row limit for a single query. For CAISO, I need to filter the LMP dataset down to just the SP15 location since there are many more locations available.

I'll pull both datasets with hourly resampling for the full 2024 year to keep things manageable - ERCOT filtered to LZ_WEST and CAISO filtered to TH_SP15_GEN-APND. This hourly granularity also aligns well with the generation profile data I'll be merging in. I'm setting up the API queries for both datasets with location filters and hourly resampling for the full year, which should keep both under the 50K row limit at around 8,784 rows each.

steps.content: Both full-year price datasets are pulled (8,784 hours each - correct for 2024 leap year). Now let me run the revenue calculations in Python:

tool_input.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# ── 1. Load generation profiles ────────────────────────────────────────────────
wind_df  = pd.read_csv("/work/wind_profile_20260619_111941.csv")
solar_df = pd.read_csv("/work/solar_profile_20260619_111944.csv")

print("Wind columns:", wind_df.columns.tolist())
print(wind_df.head(3))
print("\nSolar columns:", solar_df.columns.tolist())
print(solar_df.head(3))

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import ast

# ── 1. Parse generation profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def parse_gen_profile(path):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    df["cf"] = df["value"].apply(lambda x: ast.literal_eval(x)["electricity"])
    df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"].astype(float), unit="ms", utc=True)
    df = df[["timestamp", "cf"]].set_index("timestamp")
    return df

wind_gen  = parse_gen_profile("/work/wind_profile_20260619_111941.csv")
solar_gen = parse_gen_profile("/work/solar_profile_20260619_111944.csv")

print("Wind  rows:", len(wind_gen),  "  example:", wind_gen.head(3))
print("Solar rows:", len(solar_gen), "  example:", solar_gen.head(3))
print("Wind  annual CF:", wind_gen["cf"].mean())
print("Solar annual CF:", solar_gen["cf"].mean())

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import ast

# ── 1. Parse generation profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def parse_gen_profile(path):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    df["cf"] = df["value"].apply(lambda x: ast.literal_eval(x)["electricity"])
    df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"].astype(float), unit="ms", utc=True)
    df = df[["timestamp", "cf"]].set_index("timestamp")
    return df

wind_gen  = parse_gen_profile("/work/wind_profile_20260619_111941.csv")
solar_gen = parse_gen_profile("/work/solar_profile_20260619_111944.csv")

# ── 2. Load price data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ercot_px = pd.read_csv("/work/ercot_spp_real_time_15_min_20260619_112031.csv",
                       parse_dates=["interval_start_utc"])
ercot_px = ercot_px.set_index("interval_start_utc")[["spp"]]
ercot_px.index = ercot_px.index.tz_convert("UTC")

caiso_px = pd.read_csv("/work/caiso_lmp_real_time_15_min_20260619_112054.csv",
                       parse_dates=["interval_start_utc"])
caiso_px = caiso_px.set_index("interval_start_utc")[["lmp"]]
caiso_px.index = caiso_px.index.tz_convert("UTC")

print("ERCOT price rows:", len(ercot_px), "  NaN:", ercot_px["spp"].isna().sum())
print("CAISO price rows:", len(caiso_px), "  NaN:", caiso_px["lmp"].isna().sum())
print("\nERCOT price sample:\n", ercot_px.head(3))
print("\nCAISO price sample:\n", caiso_px.head(3))

# ── 3. Align on exact hourly timestamps ─────────────────────────────────────────
# The grid status price data has already been averaged to hourly intervals,
# where index = interval_start_utc for the hour.
# Renewables.ninja also returns UTC hourly.
# ERCOT: SPP is based on CST (UTC-6), so midnight CST = 06:00 UTC
# But Ninja returns UTC consistently.

# Merge wind + ERCOT prices
wind_merged = wind_gen.join(ercot_px, how="inner")
solar_merged = solar_gen.join(caiso_px, how="inner")

print("\nWind merged rows:", len(wind_merged))
print("Solar merged rows:", len(solar_merged))
print("\nWind merged head:\n", wind_merged.head(3))
print("\nSolar merged head:\n", solar_merged.head(3))

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import ast

# ── 1. Parse generation profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def parse_gen_profile(path):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    df["cf"] = df["value"].apply(lambda x: ast.literal_eval(x)["electricity"])
    df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"].astype(float), unit="ms", utc=True)
    df = df[["timestamp", "cf"]].set_index("timestamp")
    return df

wind_gen  = parse_gen_profile("/work/wind_profile_20260619_111941.csv")
solar_gen = parse_gen_profile("/work/solar_profile_20260619_111944.csv")

# ── 2. Load price data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ercot_px = pd.read_csv("/work/ercot_spp_real_time_15_min_20260619_112031.csv",
                       parse_dates=["interval_start_utc"])
ercot_px = ercot_px.set_index("interval_start_utc")[["spp"]]
ercot_px.index = ercot_px.index.tz_convert("UTC")

caiso_px = pd.read_csv("/work/caiso_lmp_real_time_15_min_20260619_112054.csv",
                       parse_dates=["interval_start_utc"])
caiso_px = caiso_px.set_index("interval_start_utc")[["lmp"]]
caiso_px.index = caiso_px.index.tz_convert("UTC")

# ── 3. Merge & compute hourly revenue ($ per MW of capacity, per hour) ─────────
wind_merged  = wind_gen.join(ercot_px, how="inner")
solar_merged = solar_gen.join(caiso_px, how="inner")

# Revenue per MW of nameplate capacity per hour = CF * price
# (CF is fraction of 1 MW capacity, price is $/MWh → $/MW·h)
wind_merged["rev"]  = wind_merged["cf"]  * wind_merged["spp"]
solar_merged["rev"] = solar_merged["cf"] * solar_merged["lmp"]

# ── 4. Core metrics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Annual total $ per 1 MW nameplate
wind_annual_rev   = wind_merged["rev"].sum()
solar_annual_rev  = solar_merged["rev"].sum()

# Annual generation MWh per 1 MW nameplate
wind_annual_mwh   = wind_merged["cf"].sum()    # = sum of hourly CF fractions
solar_annual_mwh  = solar_merged["cf"].sum()

# Capacity-weighted average price (merchant $/MWh realized by the asset)
wind_realized_px  = wind_annual_rev  / wind_annual_mwh
solar_realized_px = solar_annual_rev / solar_annual_mwh

# Simple flat average price (what an unweighted average gives)
wind_flat_avg     = wind_merged["spp"].mean()
solar_flat_avg    = solar_merged["lmp"].mean()

# Capture rates
wind_capture  = wind_realized_px  / wind_flat_avg  * 100
solar_capture = solar_realized_px / solar_flat_avg * 100

# ── 5. Price-correlation stats ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
wind_corr  = wind_merged["cf"].corr(wind_merged["spp"])
solar_corr = solar_merged["cf"].corr(solar_merged["lmp"])

print("=" * 70)
print("  ERCOT WEST WIND  (Midland TX, Vestas V90 2MW @ 80m, 1 MW nameplate)")
print("=" * 70)
print(f"  Annual generation       :  {wind_annual_mwh:,.0f} MWh/MW")
print(f"  Annual CF               :  {wind_annual_mwh/len(wind_merged)*100:.1f}%")
print(f"  Total annual revenue    :  ${wind_annual_rev:,.0f} /MW")
print(f"  Flat avg RT price       :  ${wind_flat_avg:.2f} /MWh")
print(f"  Generation-weighted px  :  ${wind_realized_px:.2f} /MWh  ← MERCHANT $/MWh")
print(f"  Capture rate            :  {wind_capture:.1f}% of flat avg")
print(f"  CF-vs-price correlation :  {wind_corr:.3f}")

print()
print("=" * 70)
print("  CAISO SP15 SOLAR  (Lancaster CA, 20° tilt south, 1 MW nameplate)")
print("=" * 70)
print(f"  Annual generation       :  {solar_annual_mwh:,.0f} MWh/MW")
print(f"  Annual CF               :  {solar_annual_mwh/len(solar_merged)*100:.1f}%")
print(f"  Total annual revenue    :  ${solar_annual_rev:,.0f} /MW")
print(f"  Flat avg RT price       :  ${solar_flat_avg:.2f} /MWh")
print(f"  Generation-weighted px  :  ${solar_realized_px:.2f} /MWh  ← MERCHANT $/MWh")
print(f"  Capture rate            :  {solar_capture:.1f}% of flat avg")
print(f"  CF-vs-price correlation :  {solar_corr:.3f}")

print()
print("=" * 70)
print("  SUMMARY: Annual revenue per MW nameplate")
print("=" * 70)
winner = "ERCOT West Wind" if wind_annual_rev > solar_annual_rev else "CAISO SP15 Solar"
diff   = abs(wind_annual_rev - solar_annual_rev)
print(f"  ERCOT West Wind  : ${wind_annual_rev:>10,.0f} /MW-year")
print(f"  CAISO SP15 Solar : ${solar_annual_rev:>10,.0f} /MW-year")
print(f"  Winner           : {winner}  (+${diff:,.0f} /MW-year)")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import ast

# ── 1. Parse generation profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def parse_gen_profile(path):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    df["cf"] = df["value"].apply(lambda x: ast.literal_eval(x)["electricity"])
    df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"].astype(float), unit="ms", utc=True)
    df = df[["timestamp", "cf"]].set_index("timestamp")
    return df

wind_gen  = parse_gen_profile("/work/wind_profile_20260619_111941.csv")
solar_gen = parse_gen_profile("/work/solar_profile_20260619_111944.csv")

# ── 2. Load price data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ercot_px = pd.read_csv("/work/ercot_spp_real_time_15_min_20260619_112031.csv",
                       parse_dates=["interval_start_utc"])
ercot_px = ercot_px.set_index("interval_start_utc")[["spp"]]
ercot_px.index = ercot_px.index.tz_convert("UTC")

caiso_px = pd.read_csv("/work/caiso_lmp_real_time_15_min_20260619_112054.csv",
                       parse_dates=["interval_start_utc"])
caiso_px = caiso_px.set_index("interval_start_utc")[["lmp"]]
caiso_px.index = caiso_px.index.tz_convert("UTC")

# ── 3. Merge ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
wind_merged  = wind_gen.join(ercot_px, how="inner")
solar_merged = solar_gen.join(caiso_px, how="inner")
wind_merged["rev"]  = wind_merged["cf"]  * wind_merged["spp"]
solar_merged["rev"] = solar_merged["cf"] * solar_merged["lmp"]

# ── 4. Seasonal breakdown ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def season(month):
    if month in [12,1,2]: return "Winter"
    elif month in [3,4,5]: return "Spring"
    elif month in [6,7,8]: return "Summer"
    else: return "Fall"

for df, label in [(wind_merged, "ERCOT West Wind"), (solar_merged, "CAISO SP15 Solar")]:
    df = df.copy()
    df.index = df.index.tz_convert("UTC")
    df["month"]  = df.index.month
    df["season"] = df["month"].apply(season)
    grp = df.groupby("season").agg(
        gen_mwh=("cf","sum"),
        revenue=("rev","sum")
    )
    grp["realized_px"] = grp["revenue"] / grp["gen_mwh"]
    print(f"\n{label} — Seasonal realized prices ($/MWh):")
    print(grp[["realized_px","gen_mwh","revenue"]].round(2).to_string())

# ── 5. Monthly avg realized price breakdown ────────────────────────────────────
print("\n\n--- Monthly realized $/MWh: ERCOT West Wind vs CAISO SP15 Solar ---")
for df, label, price_col in [(wind_merged,"Wind","spp"),(solar_merged,"Solar","lmp")]:
    df = df.copy()
    df["month"] = df.index.month
    mn = df.groupby("month").apply(
        lambda g: g["rev"].sum() / g["cf"].sum() if g["cf"].sum()>0 else np.nan
    )
    print(f"\n{label}:")
    month_names = ['Jan','Feb','Mar','Apr','May','Jun','Jul','Aug','Sep','Oct','Nov','Dec']
    for i, v in mn.items():
        print(f"  {month_names[i-1]}: ${v:.2f}/MWh")

# ── 6. Price depression stats for solar ───────────────────────────────────────
solar_gen_hours = solar_merged[solar_merged["cf"] > 0.01]
solar_off_hours  = solar_merged[solar_merged["cf"] <= 0.01]
print(f"\nCAISO SP15 Solar:")
print(f"  Avg price during generation hours : ${solar_gen_hours['lmp'].mean():.2f}/MWh")
print(f"  Avg price during nighttime hours  : ${solar_off_hours['lmp'].mean():.2f}/MWh")
print(f"  Hours with negative price + gen   : {(solar_gen_hours['lmp'] < 0).sum()}")
print(f"  Avg negative price during gen hrs : ${solar_gen_hours[solar_gen_hours['lmp']<0]['lmp'].mean():.2f}/MWh")

wind_pos = wind_merged[wind_merged["cf"] > 0.05]
print(f"\nERCOT West Wind:")
print(f"  Avg price during >5% CF hours     : ${wind_pos['spp'].mean():.2f}/MWh")
print(f"  Hours with negative price + gen   : {(wind_pos['spp'] < 0).sum()}")
print(f"  Avg negative price during gen hrs : ${wind_pos[wind_pos['spp']<0]['spp'].mean():.2f}/MWh if any")

steps.content:
All the data is in. Here is the full, detailed analysis:

---

# 🌬️ ERCOT West Wind vs. ☀️ CAISO SP15 Solar — 2024 Merchant Revenue Analysis

## Methodology & Assumptions
| Parameter | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|---|---|---|
| Location | Midland, TX (31.997°N, -102.078°W) | Lancaster, CA (34.68°N, -118.14°W) |
| Generation Profile | Renewables.ninja – Vestas V90 2MW @ 80m hub | Renewables.ninja – 1 kW, 20° tilt, south-facing |
| Prices | ERCOT RT 15-min SPP `LZ_WEST` (GridStatus) | CAISO RT 15-min LMP `TH_SP15_GEN-APND` (GridStatus) |
| Year | Full calendar year 2024 (8,784 hours) | Full calendar year 2024 (8,784 hours) |
| Normalized to | 1 MW nameplate | 1 MW nameplate |
| No curtailment assumed | ✅ | ✅ |

---

## 📊 Head-to-Head Summary

| Metric | 🌬️ ERCOT West Wind | ☀️ CAISO SP15 Solar |
|---|---|---|
| **Annual Generation** | **3,681 MWh/MW** | **1,868 MWh/MW** |
| **Annual Capacity Factor** | **41.9%** | **21.3%** |
| **Flat Average RT Price** | $35.69/MWh | $30.99/MWh |
| **🔑 Generation-Weighted Realized Price** | **$28.31/MWh** | **$7.32/MWh** |
| **Price Capture Rate** | **79.3% of flat avg** | **23.6% of flat avg** |
| **CF vs. Price Correlation** | -0.142 (mild) | -0.496 (severe) |
| **Annual Revenue/MW** | **$104,206/MW** | **$13,665/MW** |

> **🏆 Winner: ERCOT West Wind by a massive margin — $104,206/MW-yr vs. $13,665/MW-yr, nearly 7.6× more revenue.**

---

## 💡 Why the Realized $/MWh Tells the Full Story

The relevant comparison for a merchant asset is **generation-weighted realized price** ($/MWh), not the flat average price:

| | Wind | Solar |
|---|---|---|
| **Flat avg market price** | $35.69/MWh | $30.99/MWh |
| **Realized merchant $/MWh** | **$28.31/MWh** | **$7.32/MWh** |
| **Discount to flat** | -21% | **-76%** |

**Solar massively undersells itself** because it generates exactly when the market is flooded with solar — a textbook **price cannibalization / merit order effect**.

---

## 🗓️ Seasonal Realized Prices ($/MWh)

| Season | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|---|---|---|
| **Winter** | $24.85 | $17.90 |
| **Spring** | $28.87 | **-$21.63** ⚠️ |
| **Summer** | $30.90 | $21.79 |
| **Fall** | $28.54 | $15.96 |

Spring in CAISO SP15 was catastrophic for solar — the duck curve was deepest, with massive afternoon solar oversupply driving prices deeply negative precisely when solar output was highest.

## 📅 Monthly Realized Prices

| Month | Wind ($/MWh) | Solar ($/MWh) |
|---|---|---|
| Jan | $25.82 | $29.17 |
| Feb | $15.15 | **-$0.85** |
| Mar | $31.44 | **-$21.07** |
| Apr | $18.22 | **-$25.99** |
| May | $37.88 | **-$18.16** |
| Jun | $23.65 | $9.32 |
| Jul | $24.68 | $31.86 |
| Aug | $46.65 | $24.45 |
| Sep | $30.84 | $18.48 |
| Oct | $25.15 | $20.24 |
| Nov | $30.44 | $7.35 |
| Dec | $35.79 | $25.28 |

---

## 🔑 Key Drivers of the Gap

### 1. Capacity Factor (Volume Effect)
ERCOT West is one of the best wind regions in the US. At **41.9% CF**, the wind asset generates **~2× more energy** per MW of capacity than the solar asset (21.3% CF). This alone roughly doubles revenue.

### 2. Severe Solar Price Cannibalization in CAISO (Price Effect)
- CAISO SP15 solar prices during **generation hours averaged only $16.36/MWh**, vs. **$44.57/MWh at night** — a 63% discount.
- **1,183 generation hours** saw negative prices in 2024 (averaging **-$22.09/MWh**), costing revenue in the Spring especially.
- CF-vs-price correlation of **-0.496** for solar (vs. -0.142 for wind) confirms severe structural price depression.

### 3. ERCOT West Wind Has Better Price Correlation
Wind in West Texas correlates far less negatively with prices. August saw a **$46.65/MWh** realized price — winds pick up in the evening/nighttime when solar is offline, and ERCOT West can sometimes benefit from scarcity events. Only 687 generation hours went negative (avg -$3.35/MWh), a far smaller drag than solar.

---

## 📝 Caveats
- Revenue figures are **pre-curtailment** as requested (real-world ERCOT West wind curtailment runs ~10-20%, which would reduce wind revenues further).
- No transmission costs, ancillary services, or capacity payments are included.
- Wind profile uses a Vestas V90 2MW turbine as a representative West Texas unit; actual asset revenue depends on specific turbine and location.
- Sources: [Renewables.ninja](https://renewables.ninja) for generation profiles; [GridStatus.io](https://gridstatus.io) for ERCOT RT SPP `LZ_WEST` and CAISO RT LMP `TH_SP15_GEN-APND`.
